name: Booming Blade: on_voluntary_move Trigger Damage
description: Tests the on_voluntary_move trigger mechanic. A spell applies a 'if you move voluntarily, take thunder damage' effect to a target. When the target moves on its turn, the movement-trigger-resolver fires, dealing damage and removing the effect. This validates the generalized movement-triggered effect system used by spells like Booming Blade.

input.text: I prepare my blade with thunder energy

actions.comment: Cast Thunderous Ward on the Goblin — applies an on_voluntary_move effect via buff/debuff handler. The goblin now has an ActiveEffect: if it moves voluntarily, it takes 2d8 thunder damage.

input.text: cast Thunderous Ward at Goblin Scout

actions.comment: End PC turn — Goblin Scout's turn starts. AI 'approach' behavior makes goblin try to move toward Kael. Moving voluntarily fires the on_voluntary_move trigger from Thunderous Ward: 2d8 thunder damage (avg 9, min 2, max 16). If goblin survives, it continues moving.
Goblin should have taken thunder damage from moving voluntarily (2d8 = 2-16). With 30 HP max, HP should be less than 30. (Using average damage: ~30-9=21 expected HP.)
